name: Kit Farrow, the Mountain Scout
role: Young explorer who found the new pass
voiceHints: Fast talker, mid-twenties, always half-packed to leave. Sketches maps on any available surface. Enthusiasm outruns caution by a comfortable margin.
knowledge: Every trail, cave, and shortcut in the valley walls
The new mountain pass — and the fresh boot prints on the far side that don't match any valley maker
Saw lights moving in the Disputed Zone at night, in patterns — like a survey grid
Knows which fisher families upstream got sick first, and in what order
wants: Backing to mount a real expedition through the pass before winter — or before whoever is on the other side comes through first.
fears: Being grounded by the elders. Also, privately: the survey-grid lights weren't Harmonic OR Radiant work.
appearance: Nineteen or twenty, lean and long-legged, freckled across the nose and shoulders, red-brown hair in a messy tail. Sunburn peeling on the neck. Boots far better than the rest of the clothing.
